title: fraxinus americana subsp. texensis
intro: Fraxinus americana subsp. texensis, also known as the Texas ash, is a subspecies of the white ash tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Fraxinus americana subsp. texensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and soothe digestive issues, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, can be made by using the bark, leaves, or roots, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any plant-based remedy.
